text: ŠĻĘ√£d . By the Quran, full of reminders!
ňĻThis is the truth,ňļ yet the disbelievers are ňĻentrenchedňļ in arrogance and opposition.
ňĻImagineňļ how many peoples We destroyed before them, and they cried out when it was too late to escape.
Now, the pagans are astonished that a warner has come to them from among themselves. <sup foot_note=936>936</sup> And the disbelievers say, ‚ÄúThis is a magician, a total liar!

foot_notes.936: The pagans demanded an angel to deliver the message, not a human being like themselves.

text: Has he reduced ňĻallňļ the gods to One God? Indeed, this is something totally astonishing.‚ÄĚ
The chiefs among them went forth saying, ‚ÄúCarry on, and stand firm in devotion to your gods. Certainly this is just a scheme ňĻfor powerňļ. <sup foot_note=937>937</sup>

foot_notes.937: They meant that the Prophet ( Ô∑ļ ) is not concerned with their guidance, and that he only wants to gain dominance over them.

text: We have never heard of this in the previous faith. <sup foot_note=938>938</sup> This is nothing but a fabrication.

foot_notes.938: This refers either to the concept of the Trinity in Christianity or Arab polytheistic beliefs.

text: Has the Reminder been revealed ňĻonlyňļ to him out of ňĻall ofňļ us?‚ÄĚ In fact, they are ňĻonlyňļ in doubt of My ňĻrevealedňļ Reminder. <sup foot_note=939>939</sup> In fact, ňĻthey do so becauseňļ they have not yet tasted My punishment.

foot_notes.939: In other words, they do not question the honesty of the Prophet ( Ô∑ļ ) , but question the Quran itself. This verse is similar to 6:33.

text: Or ňĻis it becauseňļ they possess the treasuries of the mercy of your Lord‚ÄĒthe Almighty, the Giver ňĻof all bountiesňļ.
Or ňĻis it becauseňļ the kingdom of the heavens and the earth and everything in between belongs to them? Let them then climb their way ňĻto heaven, if their claim is trueňļ.
This is just another ňĻenemyňļ force bound for defeat out there. <sup foot_note=940>940</sup>

foot_notes.940: This verse alludes to the defeat of the Meccan pagans later at Badr.

text: Before them, the people of Noah denied ňĻthe truthňļ, as did ‚Äô√ād, Pharaoh of the mighty structures, <sup foot_note=941>941</sup>
Tham√Ľd, the people of Lot, and the residents of the Forest. <sup foot_note=942>942</sup> These were ňĻallňļ enemy forces.

foot_notes.942: i.e., the people of Shu‚Äôaib ( Ô∑ļ ) .

text: Each rejected their messenger, so My punishment was justified.
These ňĻpagansňļ are awaiting nothing but a single Blast that cannot be stopped.
They say ňĻmockinglyňļ, ‚ÄúOur Lord! Hasten for us our share ňĻof the punishmentňļ before the Day of Reckoning.‚ÄĚ
Be patient ňĻO Prophetňļ with what they say. And remember Our servant, David, the man of strength. Indeed, he ňĻconstantlyňļ turned ňĻto Allahňļ.
We truly subjected the mountains to hymn ňĻOur praisesňļ along with him in the evening and after sunrise.
And ňĻWe subjectedňļ the birds, flocking together. All turned to him ňĻechoing his hymnsňļ.
We strengthened his kingship, and gave him wisdom and sound judgment.
Has the story of the two plaintiffs, who scaled the ňĻwall of David‚Äôsňļ sanctuary, reached you ňĻO Prophetňļ?

When they came into David‚Äôs presence, he was startled by them. They said, ‚ÄúHave no fear. ňĻWe are merelyňļ two in a dispute: one of us has wronged the other. So judge between us with truth‚ÄĒdo not go beyond ňĻitňļ‚ÄĒand guide us to the right way.
This is my brother. <sup foot_note=943>943</sup> He has ninety-nine sheep while I have ňĻonlyňļ one. ňĻStillňļ he asked me to give it up to him, overwhelming me with ňĻhisňļ argument.‚ÄĚ

foot_notes.943: Brother in faith or business partner.

text: David ňĻeventuallyňļ ruled, ‚ÄúHe has definitely wronged you in demanding ňĻto addňļ your sheep to his. And certainly many partners wrong each other, except those who believe and do good‚ÄĒbut how few are they!‚ÄĚ Then David realized that We had tested him so he asked for his Lord‚Äôs forgiveness, fell down in prostration, and turned ňĻto Him in repentanceňļ. <sup foot_note=944>944</sup>

foot_notes.944: The verses do not offer a reason why David ( Ô∑ļ ) asked for Allah‚Äôs forgiveness. He either did so because he did not make himself available to judge between people or because he entertained some evil thoughts about the two men, thinking they actually came to assassinate him. Still he listened to both of them and made a fair judgment.

text: So We forgave that for him. And he will indeed have ňĻa status ofňļ closeness to Us and an honourable destination!

ňĻWe instructed him:ňļ ‚ÄúO David! We have surely made you an authority in the land, so judge between people with truth. And do not follow ňĻyourňļ desires or they will lead you astray from Allah‚Äôs Way. Surely those who go astray from Allah‚Äôs Way will suffer a severe punishment for neglecting the Day of Reckoning.‚ÄĚ
We have not created the heavens and earth and everything in between without purpose‚ÄĒas the disbelievers think. So woe to the disbelievers because of the Fire!
Or should We treat those who believe and do good like those who make mischief throughout the land? Or should We treat the righteous like the wicked?
ňĻThis isňļ a blessed Book which We have revealed to you ňĻO Prophetňļ so that they may contemplate its verses, and people of reason may be mindful.
And We blessed David with Solomon‚ÄĒwhat an excellent servant ňĻhe wasňļ! Indeed, he ňĻconstantlyňļ turned ňĻto Allahňļ.
ňĻRememberňļ when the well-trained, swift horses were paraded before him in the evening.
He then proclaimed, ‚ÄúI am truly in love with ňĻtheseňļ fine things out of remembrance for Allah,‚ÄĚ until they went out of sight.
ňĻHe ordered,ňļ ‚ÄúBring them back to me!‚ÄĚ Then he began to rub down their legs and necks. <sup foot_note=945>945</sup>

foot_notes.945: Some commentators have a different interpretation, which is not supported by reliable sources. They maintain that Solomon kept watching these fine horses until the sun set, then he realized that he had missed the noon prayer because of his love for horses, so he killed them, striking at their legs and necks.

text: And indeed, We tested Solomon, placing a ňĻdeformedňļ body on his throne, <sup foot_note=946>946</sup> then he turned ňĻto Allah in repentanceňļ.

foot_notes.946: It is reported in a Šł•ad√ģth collected by Bukh√Ęri that one night Solomon said that each of his wives would conceive a boy who would struggle in Allah‚Äôs cause. He forgot to say ‚ÄėAllah willing,‚ÄĚ so only one of them gave birth to a deformed, dead baby. So Solomon prayed to Allah for forgiveness.

text: He prayed, ‚ÄúMy Lord! Forgive me, and grant me an authority that will never be matched by anyone after me. You are indeed the Giver ňĻof all bountiesňļ.‚ÄĚ
So We subjected to him the wind, blowing gently at his command to wherever he pleased.
And ňĻWe subjected to himňļ every builder and diver <sup foot_note=947>947</sup> of the jinn,

foot_notes.947: The jinn dived to bring him pearls.

text: and others bound together in chains.
ňĻAllah said,ňļ ‚ÄúThis is Our gift, so give or withhold ňĻas you wishňļ, never to be called to account.‚ÄĚ
And he will indeed have ňĻa status ofňļ closeness to Us and an honourable destination!
And remember Our servant Job, when he cried out to his Lord, ‚ÄúSatan has afflicted me with distress and suffering.‚ÄĚ
ňĻWe responded,ňļ ‚ÄúStomp your foot: ňĻnowňļ here is a cool ňĻand refreshingňļ spring for washing and drinking.‚ÄĚ
And We gave him back his family, twice as many, as a mercy from Us and a lesson for people of reason.

ňĻAnd We said to him,ňļ ‚ÄúTake in your hand a bundle of grass, and strike ňĻyour wifeňļ with it, and do not break your oath.‚ÄĚ <sup foot_note=948>948</sup> We truly found him patient. What an excellent servant ňĻhe wasňļ! Indeed, he ňĻconstantlyňļ turned ňĻto Allahňļ.

foot_notes.948: Job was afflicted with losing his children, community, and with a long illness. Only his wife stayed with him. He once became angry with his wife and vowed to give her one hundred lashes if he recovered. When Job‚Äôs affliction was lifted, he became remorseful at what he had said. To help Job fulfil his vow without harming his wife, Allah ordered him to strike her with a bundle of one hundred blades of grass.

text: And remember Our servants: Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob‚ÄĒthe men of strength and insight.
We truly chose them for the honour of proclaiming the Hereafter.
And in Our sight they are truly among the chosen and the finest.
Also remember Ishmael, Elisha, and ‚Īęul-Kifl. <sup foot_note=949>949</sup> All are among the best.

foot_notes.949: Scholars are in disagreement as to whether ‚Īęul-Kifl was a prophet or just a righteous man. Those who maintain that he was a prophet identify him with various Biblical prophets such as Ezekiel, Isaiah, and Obadiah.

text: This is ňĻallňļ a reminder. And the righteous will certainly have an honourable destination:
the Gardens of Eternity, whose gates will be open for them.
There they will recline, calling for abundant fruit and drink.
And with them will be maidens of modest gaze and equal age.
This is what you are promised for the Day of Reckoning.
This is indeed Our provision that will never end.
That is that. And the transgressors will certainly have the worst destination:
Hell, where they will burn. What an evil place to rest!
Let them then taste this: boiling water and ňĻoozingňļ pus,
and other torments of the same sort!

ňĻThe misleaders will say to one another,ňļ ‚ÄúHere is a crowd ňĻof followersňļ being thrown in with us. They are not welcome, ňĻforňļ they ňĻtooňļ will burn in the Fire.‚ÄĚ <sup foot_note=950>950</sup>

foot_notes.950: Meaning, they are not welcome since their presence in Hell with us will not benefit us in anyway.

text: The followers will respond, ‚ÄúNo! You are not welcome! You brought this upon us. What an evil place for settlement!‚ÄĚ
Adding, ‚ÄúOur Lord! Whoever brought this upon us, double their punishment in the Fire.‚ÄĚ
The tyrants will ask ňĻone anotherňļ, ‚ÄúBut why do we not see those we considered to be lowly?
Were we wrong in mocking them ňĻin the worldňļ? <sup foot_note=951>951</sup> Or do our eyes ňĻjustňļ fail to see them ňĻin the Fireňļ?‚ÄĚ

foot_notes.951: In other words, did we underestimate them?

text: This dispute between the residents of the Fire will certainly come to pass.
Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúI am only a warner. And there is no god ňĻworthy of worshipňļ except Allah‚ÄĒthe One, the Supreme.
ňĻHe is theňļ Lord of the heavens and the earth and everything in between‚ÄĒthe Almighty, Most Forgiving.‚ÄĚ
Say, ‚ÄúThis ňĻQuranňļ is momentous news,
from which you ňĻpagansňļ are turning away.‚ÄĚ
ňĻAnd say,ňļ ‚ÄúI had no knowledge of the highest assembly ňĻin heavenňļ when they differed ňĻconcerning Adamňļ. <sup foot_note=952>952</sup>

foot_notes.952: When the angels obeyed Allah‚Äôs orders to prostrate before Adam, whereas Satan refused to comply.

text: What is revealed to me is that I am only sent with a clear warning.‚ÄĚ
ňĻRemember, O Prophetňļ when your Lord said to the angels, ‚ÄúI am going to create a human being from clay.
So when I have fashioned him and had a spirit of My Own ňĻcreationňļ breathed into him, fall down in prostration to him.‚ÄĚ
So the angels prostrated all together‚ÄĒ
but not Ibl√ģs , <sup foot_note=953>953</sup> who acted arrogantly, <sup foot_note=954>954</sup> becoming unfaithful.

foot_notes.954: The command to bow down was a test of obedience. Satan arrogantly refused to comply because he believed he was superior to Adam ( Ô∑ļ ) .

text: Allah asked, ‚ÄúO Ibl√ģs ! What prevented you from prostrating to what I created with My Own Hands? Did you ňĻjustňļ become proud? Or have you always been arrogant?‚ÄĚ
He replied, ‚ÄúI am better than he is: You created me from fire and him from clay.‚ÄĚ
Allah commanded, ‚ÄúThen get out of Paradise, for you are truly cursed.
And surely upon you is My condemnation until the Day of Judgment.‚ÄĚ
Satan appealed, ‚ÄúMy Lord! Then delay my end until the Day of their resurrection.‚ÄĚ
Allah said, ‚ÄúYou will be delayed
until the appointed Day.‚ÄĚ
Satan said, ‚ÄúBy Your Glory! I will certainly mislead them all,
except Your chosen servants among them.‚ÄĚ
Allah concluded, ‚ÄúThe truth is‚ÄĒand I ňĻonlyňļ say the truth‚ÄĒ:
I will surely fill up Hell with you and whoever follows you from among them, all together.‚ÄĚ
Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúI do not ask you for any reward for this ňĻQuranňļ, nor do I pretend to be someone I am not.
It is only a reminder to the whole world.
And you will certainly know its truth before long.‚ÄĚ
